calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-23-2024 Vacated

Amended Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees On Appeal
Off Calendar May-18-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF BRITANI DAVIS's Motion For Attorneys Fees On Appeal.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Britani Davis's motion for attorney's fees on appeal is DENIED.

Plaintiff's motion is based on Government Code section 12965(b) and California Rules of Court, rule 3.1702(c). Government Code section 12965(b) has no application to this motion. Section 12965(c)(6), however, provides, "In civil actions brought under this section, the court, in its discretion, may award to the prevailing party, including the department, reasonable attorney's fees and costs, including expert witness fees, except that, notwithstanding Section 998 of the Code of Civil Procedure, a prevailing defendant shall not be awarded fees and costs unless the court finds the action was frivolous, unreasonable, or groundless when brought, or the plaintiff continued to litigate after it clearly became so." In order to obtain fees under this section, a party must be the prevailing party on the merits of the FEHA claim. Here, Davis's FEHA claim has not yet been adjudicated, thus the court cannot deem her the prevailing party. Rule 3.1702 sets forth rules for claiming fees. It is not, however, a substantive fee provision. Davis's argument based on the Court of Appeal's costs award is, similarly, unavailing. A party is not entitled to recover attorney's fees on appeal based on an award of "costs" on appeal. (Stratton v. Beck (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 901.) Davis not having established a contractual or statutory right to fees at this time, the motion must be denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys' Fees On Appeal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Mar-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF BRITANI DAVIS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys' Fees On Appeal

No appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF BRITANI DAVIS' Motion For Attorneys' Fees On Appeal, as follows:

Plaintiff concedes error in the timeliness of filing her "motion for attorneys' fees on appeal" and proposes she file an "amended notice" for "another date." (Rply. 5:25-28.) It is so ordered.

Further, plaintiff's reply brief adds a new argument based on the court of appeal's statement that she "shall recover her costs on appeal." (See, e.g., Rply. 5:7, 6:6.) As this argument was not made in plaintiff's opening memorandum, defendant may file a five-page supplemental brief on that point at least nine court days before the new hearing date.

Order signed after hearing.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration; Petition To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities;Declaration Of Marc Cohen; Declaration Of Gumaro Barraza; Proposed Order
Off Calendar Nov-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Elizabeth A. Medrano (EMedrano@maternlawgroup.com) for Plaintiff.
Marc Cohen (213-223-7707; marc@clgapc.com) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHIEKH SHOES, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration; Petition To Compel Arbitration;

Defendant Sheikh Shoes, LLC's petition to compel arbitration of this sexual harassment case is denied on waiver grounds. Plaintiff Britani Davis sued 19 months ago, in March 2019. Since then, defendant has answered the complaint, responded to written discovery (including meeting and conferring and filing supplemental responses), made court appearances, filed case management statements and seen a co-defendant move unsuccessfully to stay the litigation pending a criminal case. But defendant did not pursue arbitration until August 2020.
Attempting to excuse its delay, defendant says its first counsel "substituted out" in January 2020, but does not explain the preceding months or why it delayed in retaining new counsel. Defendant also notes that "non-essential court operations" paused from March 16 to June 1, 2020. However, this case is in the pre-trial phase and this court's law and motion department was deciding motions throughout, so the court cannot be blamed for defendant's delay.
Defendant thus fails the waiver test California's supreme court adopted in St. Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1196: defendant's actions were "inconsistent with the right to arbitrate;" defendant "delayed for a long period of time;" "litigation machinery has been substantially invoked;" "judicial discovery procedures" took place; and the delay "affected, misled or prejudiced" plaintiff.

order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
